personalInfo.name: MIKITA KAVALIOU
personalInfo.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer | Next.js, Node.js & PHP
personalInfo.professionalObjective: Results-driven Full-Stack Engineer with 3+ years of experience architecting scalable B2B/B2C SaaS platforms. Expert in modern React ecosystems (Next.js 15), PostgreSQL optimization, and integrating complex Generative AI workflows. Proven track record of reducing system latency by 60% and delivering high-availability applications utilizing Clean Architecture and SOLID principles.
personalInfo.status: Based in Grenoble, France. Free access to the EU job market.

experience.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
responsibilities: Architected Lexity: Built a multimodal AI ecosystem utilizing Next.js 15 and a Composite AI layer (Gemini, Groq) with automated failover logic to maintain sub-second latency.
Engineered ZIRO.FIT: Developed a B2B2C SaaS utilizing Prisma and Stripe Connect; optimized backend data retrieval via Raw SQL aggregations to reduce dashboard load times by 60%.
Developed Interly: Shipped an AI-driven interview platform featuring real-time Web Audio API transcription, 99.9% AI availability via key rotation, and a custom Spaced Repetition System (SRS).

experience.title: Full Stack Web Developer
responsibilities: Enterprise Development & Maintenance: Engineered and deployed production-ready applications from scratch utilizing Laravel, Symfony, and Next.js, while simultaneously maintaining and scaling complex legacy enterprise projects.
Performance Optimization: Refactored legacy MySQL queries and implemented Redis caching on Laravel backends, reducing API response times by 30%.
Fintech Integrations: Integrated secure Stripe billing flows including subscriptions and webhooks, contributing to a 5% increase in overall conversion rates.
Frontend Architecture: Built scalable, accessible Next.js user interfaces utilizing TypeScript, enforcing strict state management and component reusability for new feature implementations.

projects.name: Lexity: AI-Native Language Ecosystem
